revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:円筒棺]]
[[File:土師の里8号墳 円筒棺.JPG|thumb|250x250px|{{center|Cylindrical Coffin from Hani no Sato No. 8 Kofun}}{{small|Displayed at {{ill|Aisel Schura Hall|ja|アイセルシュラホール|1=AICEL Shura Hall|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aisel Schura Hall|13=User:Immanuelle/AICEL Shura Hall|qq=}}.}}]] '''Cylindrical Coffin''' (円筒棺, entōkan) refers to a coffin made of terracotta materials or cylindrical haniwa. It is also known as a '''haniwa coffin''', '''cylindrical haniwa coffin''', or '''haniwa cylindrical coffin'''.
== Overview ==
When cylindrical coffins are specifically crafted as coffins, they are typically long, singular cylinders with multiple convex bands encircling them, and both ends are capped with dome-shaped terracotta lids. These are generally buried horizontally, with the deceased laid inside in an extended burial position ({{ill|stretched burial|ja|伸展葬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stretched burial|qq=}}).
In cases where cylindrical haniwa are repurposed, two flat-topped haniwa, usually from morning glory-shaped cylindrical haniwa, are utilized. Their bases are joined to create a single coffin, resembling the structure of joined jar coffins. The ends and openings of the cylindrical haniwa are sealed with fragments of haniwa. These coffins were also likely buried horizontally for extended burials.
Artifacts such as antler-made sword fittings found in the cylindrical coffin of {{ill|Konai Kofun|ja|近内古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konai Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135504633|1=Konai Kofun}} in Nara Prefecture and stone {{ill|Magatama|en|Magatama|qid=Q334232|lt=magatama|1=Magatama}} from the cylindrical coffin of {{ill|Yakushiyama Kofun|ja|薬師山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakushiyama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135504635|1=Yakushiyama Kofun}} in Hyōgo Prefecture suggest that this type of coffin was primarily used during the mid-Kofun period.
A distinction has been proposed where coffins made with specially crafted cylindrical components are called "haniwa coffins," while those utilizing cylindrical haniwa are termed "haniwa cylindrical coffins."
== References ==
* Seichi Mizuno and Yukio Kobayashi, *Illustrated Archaeological Dictionary*, Tokyo Sogensha, 1974, 7th edition, p. 177.
== See Also ==
* {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|qid=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} * {{ill|Sarcophagus|ja|石棺|1=Sekkan|1=Sekkan (Sarcophagus)|lt=Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekkan|qq=}} * {{ill|Wooden coffin|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin|1=Wooden Coffin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Coffin|qq=}}
